title: Capital Markets: Analyze, Evaluate & Invest
description:
By the end of this course, learners will be able to identify the functions of financial markets, differentiate between equity, debt, and derivative instruments, evaluate the pros and cons of investment tools, and analyze the role of intermediaries, stock exchanges, and regulators in capital markets.

This comprehensive program is designed to give learners a practical and structured understanding of capital markets and financial instruments. Starting from foundational concepts, it explores equity, preference shares, debentures, derivatives, and mutual funds, while also covering the infrastructure, intermediaries, and processes that ensure smooth market operations.

Learners will benefit from step-by-step lessons, structured modules, practice quizzes, and graded assessments that reinforce knowledge and build confidence. The course uniquely combines conceptual clarity with applied insights, making complex topics like derivatives, corporate actions, and trade life cycles easy to understand and apply in real-world scenarios.

Whether you are an aspiring investor, finance student, or professional looking to strengthen your market knowledge, this course equips you with the ability to analyze, evaluate, and make informed investment decisions with confidence.
